comment_text.sn12.70:2.1: This Susīma appears only here, but we meet some deities of the same name (SN 2.29, SN 11.2).
comment_text.sn12.70:3.4: There is no mention of the normal probationary period for renunciates who convert from another sect. Perhaps it is just a simple omission, or perhaps this event happened before the rule was laid down at Kd 1:38.1.1.
comment_text.sn12.70:5.1: Susīma misunderstands the role of these attainments in the path. They are higher powers which can be realized through deep meditation, but which are not required for awakening, although they can be helpful in some cases.
comment_text.sn12.70:11.2: One who is freed by wisdom develops the absorptions as a basis for insight (AN 9.44), but may not practice the formless meditations (MN 70:16.1).
comment_text.sn12.70:12.3: This reply appears unhelpful, but note that the Buddha replies the same way. The point is that humility is needed for wisdom. Susīma, with his dubious intentions and limited understanding, needs to recognize this before he can advance.
comment_text.sn12.70:13.3: The Buddha emphasizes that insight into the “natural principle” (of dependent origination) is the key to liberation.
comment_text.sn12.70:15.1: The Buddha goes on to establish Susīma in the fundamental wisdom teachings, starting with the five aggregates and three characteristics. This approach engages Susīma in question and answer, giving him confidence.
comment_text.sn12.70:18.1: The teaching on the aggregates culminates in the ending of rebirth, which the Buddha uses to segue into dependent origination.
comment_text.sn12.70:24.1: Both dependent origination and the power of clairvoyance are related to knowledge of rebirth, but in quite different ways. Clairvoyance sees the details of particular lives as they play out, like watching a show on a TV screen, whereas dependent origination understands the principles underlying the process of rebirth, like taking the back off the TV and seeing how the device works.
comment_text.sn12.70:27.2: Despite his corrupt intent in ordaining, Susīma showed a genuine interest in understanding and came to his senses. The Buddha did not confront or condemn him, but nurtured his understanding until Susīma was ready to confess of his own volition. | “Thief of the teachings” (dhammatthena) is a unique term, recalling Susīma’s initial plan to literally steal the teachings for profit. Unfortunately, such behavior is now common in the age of AI.
comment_text.sn12.70:28.1: When someone genuinely confesses, the Buddha makes a point to acknowledge what they had done, without dismissing it and thereby discounting the inner work they had achieved to get to this point.
comment_text.sn12.70:29.4: The commentary uses the discourse to argue for its theory of “dry insight” without the practice of absorption. If the Buddha had intended to teach such a radical departure from the normal path, however, he would surely have stated it clearly and not relegated it to an obscure discussion with a reformed heretic. As it stands, the discourse in no way supports the theory, nor is it found anywhere else in the early texts.

root_text.sn12.70:1.2: ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā rājagahe viharati veḷuvane kalandakanivāpe.
root_text.sn12.70:1.3: Tena kho pana samayena bhagavā sakkato hoti garukato mānito pūjito apacito lābhī cīvarapiṇḍapātasenāsanagilānappaccayabhesajjaparikkhārānaṁ.
root_text.sn12.70:1.4: Bhikkhusaṅghopi sakkato hoti garukato mānito pūjito apacito lābhī cīvarapiṇḍapātasenāsanagilānappaccayabhesajjaparikkhārānaṁ.
root_text.sn12.70:1.5: Aññatitthiyā pana paribbājakā asakkatā honti agarukatā amānitā apūjitā anapacitā, na lābhino cīvarapiṇḍapātasenāsanagilānappaccayabhesajjaparikkhārānaṁ.
root_text.sn12.70:2.1: Tena kho pana samayena susimo paribbājako rājagahe paṭivasati mahatiyā paribbājakaparisāya saddhiṁ.
root_text.sn12.70:2.2: Atha kho susimassa paribbājakassa parisā susimaṁ paribbājakaṁ etadavocuṁ:
root_text.sn12.70:2.3: “ehi tvaṁ, āvuso susima, samaṇe gotame brahmacariyaṁ cara.
root_text.sn12.70:2.5: Taṁ mayaṁ dhammaṁ pariyāpuṇitvā gihīnaṁ bhāsissāma.
root_text.sn12.70:2.6: Evaṁ mayampi sakkatā bhavissāma garukatā mānitā pūjitā apacitā lābhino cīvarapiṇḍapātasenāsanagilānappaccayabhesajjaparikkhārānan”ti.
root_text.sn12.70:2.7: “Evamāvuso”ti kho susimo paribbājako sakāya parisāya paṭissuṇitvā yenāyasmā ānando tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā āyasmatā ānandena saddhiṁ sammodi.
root_text.sn12.70:2.8: Sammodanīyaṁ kathaṁ sāraṇīyaṁ vītisāretvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. Ekamantaṁ nisinno kho susimo paribbājako āyasmantaṁ ānandaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn12.70:2.9: “icchāmahaṁ, āvuso ānanda, imasmiṁ dhammavinaye brahmacariyaṁ caritun”ti.
root_text.sn12.70:3.1: Atha kho āyasmā ānando susimaṁ paribbājakaṁ ādāya yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. Ekamantaṁ nisinno kho āyasmā ānando bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn12.70:3.3: ‘icchāmahaṁ, āvuso ānanda, imasmiṁ dhammavinaye brahmacariyaṁ caritun’”ti.
root_text.sn12.70:3.5: Alattha kho susimo paribbājako bhagavato santike pabbajjaṁ, alattha upasampadaṁ.
root_text.sn12.70:4.1: Tena kho pana samayena sambahulehi bhikkhūhi bhagavato santike aññā byākatā hoti:
root_text.sn12.70:4.2: “khīṇā jāti, vusitaṁ brahmacariyaṁ, kataṁ karaṇīyaṁ, nāparaṁ itthattāyāti pajānāmā”ti.
root_text.sn12.70:4.4: “sambahulehi kira bhikkhūhi bhagavato santike aññā byākatā:
root_text.sn12.70:4.5: ‘khīṇā jāti, vusitaṁ brahmacariyaṁ, kataṁ karaṇīyaṁ, nāparaṁ itthattāyā’ti pajānāmā”ti.
root_text.sn12.70:4.6: Atha kho āyasmā susimo yena te bhikkhū tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā tehi bhikkhūhi saddhiṁ sammodi.
root_text.sn12.70:4.7: Sammodanīyaṁ kathaṁ sāraṇīyaṁ vītisāretvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. Ekamantaṁ nisinno kho āyasmā susimo te bhikkhū etadavoca:
root_text.sn12.70:4.9: ‘khīṇā jāti, vusitaṁ brahmacariyaṁ, kataṁ karaṇīyaṁ, nāparaṁ itthattāyā’ti pajānāmā”ti?
root_text.sn12.70:5.1: “Api pana tumhe āyasmanto evaṁ jānantā evaṁ passantā anekavihitaṁ iddhividhaṁ paccanubhotha—ekopi hutvā bahudhā hotha, bahudhāpi hutvā eko hotha; āvibhāvaṁ, tirobhāvaṁ, tirokuṭṭaṁ tiropākāraṁ tiropabbataṁ asajjamānā gacchatha, seyyathāpi ākāse; pathaviyāpi ummujjanimujjaṁ karotha, seyyathāpi udake; udakepi abhijjamāne gacchatha, seyyathāpi pathaviyaṁ; ākāsepi pallaṅkena kamatha, seyyathāpi pakkhī sakuṇo; imepi candimasūriye evaṁmahiddhike evaṁmahānubhāve pāṇinā parimasatha parimajjatha, yāva brahmalokāpi kāyena vasaṁ vattethā”ti?
root_text.sn12.70:6.1: “Api pana tumhe āyasmanto evaṁ jānantā evaṁ passantā dibbāya sotadhātuyā visuddhāya atikkantamānusikāya ubho sadde suṇātha dibbe ca mānuse ca ye dūre santike cā”ti?
root_text.sn12.70:7.1: “Api pana tumhe āyasmanto evaṁ jānantā evaṁ passantā parasattānaṁ parapuggalānaṁ cetasā ceto paricca pajānātha—
root_text.sn12.70:7.2: sarāgaṁ vā cittaṁ sarāgaṁ cittanti pajānātha;
root_text.sn12.70:7.3: vītarāgaṁ vā cittaṁ vītarāgaṁ cittanti pajānātha;
root_text.sn12.70:7.4: sadosaṁ vā cittaṁ sadosaṁ cittanti pajānātha;
root_text.sn12.70:7.5: vītadosaṁ vā cittaṁ vītadosaṁ cittanti pajānātha;
root_text.sn12.70:7.6: samohaṁ vā cittaṁ samohaṁ cittanti pajānātha;
root_text.sn12.70:7.7: vītamohaṁ vā cittaṁ vītamohaṁ cittanti pajānātha;
root_text.sn12.70:7.8: saṅkhittaṁ vā cittaṁ saṅkhittaṁ cittanti pajānātha;
root_text.sn12.70:7.9: vikkhittaṁ vā cittaṁ vikkhittaṁ cittanti pajānātha;
root_text.sn12.70:7.10: mahaggataṁ vā cittaṁ mahaggataṁ cittanti pajānātha;
root_text.sn12.70:7.11: amahaggataṁ vā cittaṁ amahaggataṁ cittanti pajānātha;
root_text.sn12.70:7.12: sauttaraṁ vā cittaṁ sauttaraṁ cittanti pajānātha;
root_text.sn12.70:7.13: anuttaraṁ vā cittaṁ anuttaraṁ cittanti pajānātha;
root_text.sn12.70:7.14: samāhitaṁ vā cittaṁ samāhitaṁ cittanti pajānātha;
root_text.sn12.70:7.15: asamāhitaṁ vā cittaṁ asamāhitaṁ cittanti pajānātha;
root_text.sn12.70:7.16: vimuttaṁ vā cittaṁ vimuttaṁ cittanti pajānātha;
root_text.sn12.70:7.17: avimuttaṁ vā cittaṁ avimuttaṁ cittanti pajānāthā”ti?
root_text.sn12.70:8.1: “Api pana tumhe āyasmanto evaṁ jānantā evaṁ passantā anekavihitaṁ pubbenivāsaṁ anussaratha, seyyathidaṁ—ekampi jātiṁ dvepi jātiyo tissopi jātiyo catassopi jātiyo pañcapi jātiyo dasapi jātiyo vīsampi jātiyo tiṁsampi jātiyo cattārīsampi jātiyo paññāsampi jātiyo jātisatampi jātisahassampi jātisatasahassampi, anekepi saṁvaṭṭakappe anekepi vivaṭṭakappe anekepi saṁvaṭṭavivaṭṭakappe: ‘amutrāsiṁ evaṁnāmo evaṅgotto evaṁvaṇṇo evamāhāro evaṁsukhadukkhapaṭisaṁvedī evamāyupariyanto, so tato cuto amutra udapādiṁ; tatrāpāsiṁ evaṁnāmo evaṅgotto evaṁvaṇṇo evamāhāro evaṁsukhadukkhapaṭisaṁvedī evamāyupariyanto, so tato cuto idhūpapanno’ti. Iti sākāraṁ sauddesaṁ anekavihitaṁ pubbenivāsaṁ anussarathā”ti?
root_text.sn12.70:9.1: “Api pana tumhe āyasmanto evaṁ jānantā evaṁ passantā dibbena cakkhunā visuddhena atikkantamānusakena satte passatha cavamāne upapajjamāne hīne paṇīte suvaṇṇe dubbaṇṇe, sugate duggate yathākammūpage satte pajānātha: ‘ime vata bhonto sattā kāyaduccaritena samannāgatā vacīduccaritena samannāgatā manoduccaritena samannāgatā, ariyānaṁ upavādakā micchādiṭṭhikā micchādiṭṭhikammasamādānā, te kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā apāyaṁ duggatiṁ vinipātaṁ nirayaṁ upapannā; ime vā pana bhonto sattā kāyasucaritena samannāgatā vacīsucaritena samannāgatā manosucaritena samannāgatā, ariyānaṁ anupavādakā sammādiṭṭhikā sammādiṭṭhikammasamādānā te kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā sugatiṁ saggaṁ lokaṁ upapannā’ti, iti dibbena cakkhunā visuddhena atikkantamānusakena satte passatha cavamāne upapajjamāne hīne paṇīte suvaṇṇe dubbaṇṇe, sugate duggate yathākammūpage satte pajānāthā”ti?
root_text.sn12.70:10.1: “Api pana tumhe āyasmanto evaṁ jānantā evaṁ passantā ye te santā vimokkhā atikkamma rūpe āruppā, te kāyena phusitvā viharathā”ti?
root_text.sn12.70:11.1: “Ettha dāni āyasmanto idañca veyyākaraṇaṁ imesañca dhammānaṁ asamāpatti; idaṁ no, āvuso, kathan”ti?
root_text.sn12.70:12.1: “Na khvāhaṁ imassa āyasmantānaṁ saṅkhittena bhāsitassa vitthārena atthaṁ ājānāmi.
root_text.sn12.70:12.2: Sādhu me āyasmanto tathā bhāsantu yathāhaṁ imassa āyasmantānaṁ saṅkhittena bhāsitassa vitthārena atthaṁ ājāneyyan”ti.
root_text.sn12.70:12.3: “Ājāneyyāsi vā tvaṁ, āvuso susima, na vā tvaṁ ājāneyyāsi atha kho paññāvimuttā mayan”ti.
root_text.sn12.70:13.1: Atha kho āyasmā susimo uṭṭhāyāsanā yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi.
root_text.sn12.70:13.2: Ekamantaṁ nisinno kho āyasmā susimo yāvatako tehi bhikkhūhi saddhiṁ ahosi kathāsallāpo taṁ sabbaṁ bhagavato ārocesi.
root_text.sn12.70:13.3: “Pubbe kho, susima, dhammaṭṭhitiñāṇaṁ, pacchā nibbāne ñāṇan”ti.
root_text.sn12.70:14.1: “Na khvāhaṁ, bhante, imassa bhagavatā saṅkhittena bhāsitassa vitthārena atthaṁ ājānāmi.
root_text.sn12.70:14.2: Sādhu me, bhante, bhagavā tathā bhāsatu yathāhaṁ imassa bhagavatā saṅkhittena bhāsitassa vitthārena atthaṁ ājāneyyan”ti.
root_text.sn12.70:14.3: “Ājāneyyāsi vā tvaṁ, susima, na vā tvaṁ ājāneyyāsi, atha kho dhammaṭṭhitiñāṇaṁ pubbe, pacchā nibbāne ñāṇaṁ.
root_text.sn12.70:15.4: “Yaṁ panāniccaṁ dukkhaṁ vā taṁ sukhaṁ vā”ti?
root_text.sn12.70:15.6: “Yaṁ panāniccaṁ dukkhaṁ vipariṇāmadhammaṁ, kallaṁ nu taṁ samanupassituṁ:
root_text.sn12.70:15.11: “Yaṁ panāniccaṁ dukkhaṁ vā taṁ sukhaṁ vā”ti?
root_text.sn12.70:15.13: “Yaṁ panāniccaṁ dukkhaṁ vipariṇāmadhammaṁ, kallaṁ nu taṁ samanupassituṁ:
root_text.sn12.70:15.20: “Yaṁ panāniccaṁ dukkhaṁ vā taṁ sukhaṁ vā”ti?
root_text.sn12.70:15.22: “Yaṁ panāniccaṁ dukkhaṁ vipariṇāmadhammaṁ, kallaṁ nu taṁ samanupassituṁ:
root_text.sn12.70:15.27: “Yaṁ panāniccaṁ dukkhaṁ vā taṁ sukhaṁ vā”ti?
root_text.sn12.70:15.29: “Yaṁ panāniccaṁ dukkhaṁ vipariṇāmadhammaṁ, kallaṁ nu taṁ samanupassituṁ:
root_text.sn12.70:16.1: “Tasmātiha, susima, yaṁ kiñci rūpaṁ atītānāgatapaccuppannaṁ ajjhattaṁ vā bahiddhā vā oḷārikaṁ vā sukhumaṁ vā hīnaṁ vā paṇītaṁ vā yaṁ dūre santike vā, sabbaṁ rūpaṁ netaṁ mama nesohamasmi na meso attāti; evametaṁ yathābhūtaṁ sammappaññāya daṭṭhabbaṁ.
root_text.sn12.70:16.2: Yā kāci vedanā atītānāgatapaccuppannā ajjhattaṁ vā bahiddhā vā oḷārikā vā sukhumā vā hīnā vā paṇītā vā yā dūre santike vā, sabbā vedanā netaṁ mama nesohamasmi na meso attāti; evametaṁ yathābhūtaṁ sammappaññāya daṭṭhabbaṁ.
root_text.sn12.70:16.4: ye keci saṅkhārā atītānāgatapaccuppannā ajjhattaṁ vā bahiddhā vā oḷārikā vā sukhumā vā hīnā vā paṇītā vā ye dūre santike vā, sabbe saṅkhārā netaṁ mama nesohamasmi na meso attāti; evametaṁ yathābhūtaṁ sammappaññāya daṭṭhabbaṁ.
root_text.sn12.70:16.5: Yaṁ kiñci viññāṇaṁ atītānāgatapaccuppannaṁ ajjhattaṁ vā bahiddhā vā oḷārikaṁ vā sukhumaṁ vā hīnaṁ vā paṇītaṁ vā yaṁ dūre santike vā, sabbaṁ viññāṇaṁ netaṁ mama nesohamasmi na meso attāti; evametaṁ yathābhūtaṁ sammappaññāya daṭṭhabbaṁ.
root_text.sn12.70:17.1: Evaṁ passaṁ, susima, sutavā ariyasāvako rūpasmimpi nibbindati, vedanāyapi nibbindati, saññāyapi nibbindati, saṅkhāresupi nibbindati, viññāṇasmimpi nibbindati.
root_text.sn12.70:17.2: Nibbindaṁ virajjati, virāgā vimuccati, vimuttasmiṁ vimuttamiti ñāṇaṁ hoti.
root_text.sn12.70:17.3: ‘Khīṇā jāti, vusitaṁ brahmacariyaṁ, kataṁ karaṇīyaṁ, nāparaṁ itthattāyā’ti pajānāti.
root_text.sn12.70:20.1: “Api pana tvaṁ, susima, evaṁ jānanto evaṁ passanto anekavihitaṁ iddhividhaṁ paccanubhosi—ekopi hutvā bahudhā hosi, bahudhāpi hutvā eko hosi; āvibhāvaṁ, tirobhāvaṁ, tirokuṭṭaṁ tiropākāraṁ tiropabbataṁ asajjamāno gacchasi, seyyathāpi ākāse; pathaviyāpi ummujjanimujjaṁ karosi, seyyathāpi udake; udakepi abhijjamāno gacchasi, seyyathāpi pathaviyaṁ; ākāsepi pallaṅkena kamasi, seyyathāpi pakkhī sakuṇo; imepi candimasūriye evaṁmahiddhike evaṁmahānubhāve pāṇinā parimasasi parimajjasi, yāva brahmalokāpi kāyena vasaṁ vattesī”ti?
root_text.sn12.70:21.1: “Api pana tvaṁ, susima, evaṁ jānanto evaṁ passanto dibbāya sotadhātuyā visuddhāya atikkantamānusikāya ubho sadde suṇasi dibbe ca mānuse ca ye dūre santike cā”ti?
root_text.sn12.70:22.1: “Api pana tvaṁ, susima, evaṁ jānanto evaṁ passanto parasattānaṁ parapuggalānaṁ cetasā ceto paricca pajānāsi—
root_text.sn12.70:22.2: sarāgaṁ vā cittaṁ sarāgaṁ cittanti pajānāsi …pe…
root_text.sn12.70:22.3: vimuttaṁ vā cittaṁ vimuttaṁ cittanti pajānāsī”ti?
root_text.sn12.70:23.1: “Api pana tvaṁ, susima, evaṁ jānanto evaṁ passanto anekavihitaṁ pubbenivāsaṁ anussarasi, seyyathidaṁ—ekampi jātiṁ …pe… iti sākāraṁ sauddesaṁ anekavihitaṁ pubbenivāsaṁ anussarasī”ti?
root_text.sn12.70:24.1: “Api pana tvaṁ, susima, evaṁ jānanto evaṁ passanto dibbena cakkhunā visuddhena atikkantamānusakena satte passasi cavamāne …pe… yathākammūpage satte pajānāsī”ti?
root_text.sn12.70:25.1: “Api pana tvaṁ, susima, evaṁ jānanto evaṁ passanto ye te santā vimokkhā atikkamma rūpe āruppā, te kāyena phusitvā viharasī”ti?
root_text.sn12.70:26.1: “Ettha dāni, susima, idañca veyyākaraṇaṁ imesañca dhammānaṁ asamāpatti, idaṁ no, susima, kathan”ti?
root_text.sn12.70:27.1: Atha kho āyasmā susimo bhagavato pādesu sirasā nipatitvā bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn12.70:27.2: “accayo maṁ, bhante, accagamā yathābālaṁ yathāmūḷhaṁ yathāakusalaṁ, yvāhaṁ evaṁ svākkhāte dhammavinaye dhammatthenako pabbajito.
root_text.sn12.70:27.3: Tassa me, bhante, bhagavā accayaṁ accayato paṭiggaṇhātu āyatiṁ saṁvarāyā”ti.
root_text.sn12.70:28.1: “Taggha tvaṁ, susima, accayo accagamā yathābālaṁ yathāmūḷhaṁ yathāakusalaṁ, yo tvaṁ evaṁ svākkhāte dhammavinaye dhammatthenako pabbajito.
root_text.sn12.70:28.2: Seyyathāpi, susima, coraṁ āgucāriṁ gahetvā rañño dasseyyuṁ:
root_text.sn12.70:28.3: ‘ayaṁ te, deva, coro āgucārī, imassa yaṁ icchasi taṁ daṇḍaṁ paṇehī’ti.
root_text.sn12.70:28.5: ‘gacchatha, bho, imaṁ purisaṁ daḷhāya rajjuyā pacchābāhaṁ gāḷhabandhanaṁ bandhitvā khuramuṇḍaṁ karitvā kharassarena paṇavena rathiyāya rathiyaṁ siṅghāṭakena siṅghāṭakaṁ parinetvā dakkhiṇena dvārena nikkhāmetvā dakkhiṇato nagarassa sīsaṁ chindathā’ti.
root_text.sn12.70:28.6: Tamenaṁ rañño purisā daḷhāya rajjuyā pacchābāhaṁ gāḷhabandhanaṁ bandhitvā khuramuṇḍaṁ karitvā kharassarena paṇavena rathiyāya rathiyaṁ siṅghāṭakena siṅghāṭakaṁ parinetvā dakkhiṇena dvārena nikkhāmetvā dakkhiṇato nagarassa sīsaṁ chindeyyuṁ.
root_text.sn12.70:28.8: api nu so puriso tatonidānaṁ dukkhaṁ domanassaṁ paṭisaṁvediyethā”ti?
root_text.sn12.70:29.1: “Yaṁ kho so, susima, puriso tatonidānaṁ dukkhaṁ domanassaṁ paṭisaṁvediyetha.
root_text.sn12.70:29.2: Yā evaṁ svākkhāte dhammavinaye dhammatthenakassa pabbajjā, ayaṁ tato dukkhavipākatarā ca kaṭukavipākatarā ca, api ca vinipātāya saṁvattati.
root_text.sn12.70:29.3: Yato ca kho tvaṁ, susima, accayaṁ accayato disvā yathādhammaṁ paṭikarosi taṁ te mayaṁ paṭiggaṇhāma.
root_text.sn12.70:29.4: Vuddhi hesā, susima, ariyassa vinaye yo accayaṁ accayato disvā yathādhammaṁ paṭikaroti, āyatiñca saṁvaraṁ āpajjatī”ti.

translation_text.sn12.70:1.2: At one time the Buddha was staying near Rājagaha, in the Bamboo Grove, the squirrels’ feeding ground.
translation_text.sn12.70:1.3: Now at that time the Buddha was honored, respected, revered, venerated, and esteemed. And he received robes, almsfood, lodgings, and medicines and supplies for the sick.
translation_text.sn12.70:1.4: And the mendicant Saṅgha was also honored, respected, revered, venerated, and esteemed. And they received robes, almsfood, lodgings, and medicines and supplies for the sick.
translation_text.sn12.70:1.5: But the wanderers who followed other religions were not honored, respected, revered, venerated, and esteemed. And they didn’t receive robes, almsfood, lodgings, and medicines and supplies for the sick.
translation_text.sn12.70:2.1: Now at that time the wanderer Susīma was residing at Rājagaha together with a large community of wanderers.
translation_text.sn12.70:2.2: Then his community said to Susīma,
translation_text.sn12.70:2.3: “Reverend Susīma, please lead the spiritual life with the ascetic Gotama.
translation_text.sn12.70:2.4: Memorize that teaching and have us recite it with you.
translation_text.sn12.70:2.5: When we’ve memorized it we’ll recite it to the laity.
translation_text.sn12.70:2.6: In this way we too will be honored, respected, revered, venerated, and esteemed. And we’ll receive robes, almsfood, lodgings, and medicines and supplies for the sick.”
translation_text.sn12.70:2.7: “Yes, reverends,” replied Susīma to his own assembly. Then he went to Venerable Ānanda, and exchanged greetings with him.
translation_text.sn12.70:2.8: When the greetings and polite conversation were over, he sat down to one side and said to Ānanda,
translation_text.sn12.70:2.9: “Reverend Ānanda, I wish to lead the spiritual life in this teaching and training.”
translation_text.sn12.70:3.1: Then Ānanda took Susīma to the Buddha, bowed, sat down to one side, and said to him,
translation_text.sn12.70:3.3: he wishes to lead the spiritual life in this teaching and training.”
translation_text.sn12.70:3.4: “Well then, Ānanda, give Susīma the going forth.”
translation_text.sn12.70:3.5: And the wanderer Susīma received the going forth, the ordination in the Buddha’s presence.
translation_text.sn12.70:4.1: Now at that time several mendicants had declared their enlightenment in the Buddha’s presence:
translation_text.sn12.70:4.2: “We understand: ‘Rebirth is ended, the spiritual journey has been completed, what had to be done has been done, there is nothing further for this place.’”
translation_text.sn12.70:4.6: He went up to those mendicants, and exchanged greetings with them.
translation_text.sn12.70:4.7: When the greetings and polite conversation were over, he sat down to one side and said to those mendicants,
translation_text.sn12.70:4.8: “Is it really true that the venerables have declared enlightenment in the Buddha’s presence?”
translation_text.sn12.70:5.1: “But knowing and seeing thus, do you wield the many kinds of psychic power? That is, multiplying yourselves and becoming one again; materializing and dematerializing; going unobstructed through a wall, a rampart, or a mountain as if through space; diving in and out of the earth as if it were water; walking on water as if it were earth; flying cross-legged through the sky like a bird; touching and stroking with the hand the sun and moon, so mighty and powerful. Do you control the body as far as the realm of divinity?”
translation_text.sn12.70:6.1: “Well, knowing and seeing thus do you, with clairaudience that is purified and superhuman, hear both kinds of sounds, human and heavenly, whether near or far?”
translation_text.sn12.70:7.1: “Well, knowing and seeing thus, do you understand the minds of other beings and individuals, having encompassed them with your mind?
translation_text.sn12.70:7.2: Do you understand mind with greed as ‘mind with greed’,
translation_text.sn12.70:7.3: and mind without greed as ‘mind without greed’?
translation_text.sn12.70:7.4: Do you understand mind with hate as ‘mind with hate’,
translation_text.sn12.70:7.5: and mind without hate as ‘mind without hate’?
translation_text.sn12.70:7.6: Do you understand mind with delusion as ‘mind with delusion’,
translation_text.sn12.70:7.7: and mind without delusion as ‘mind without delusion’?
translation_text.sn12.70:7.8: Do you understand constricted mind as ‘constricted mind’,
translation_text.sn12.70:7.10: Do you understand expansive mind as ‘expansive mind’,
translation_text.sn12.70:7.12: Do you understand mind that is not supreme as ‘mind that is not supreme’,
translation_text.sn12.70:7.13: and mind that is supreme as ‘mind that is supreme’?
translation_text.sn12.70:7.14: Do you understand mind immersed in samādhi as ‘mind immersed in samādhi’,
translation_text.sn12.70:7.15: and mind not immersed in samādhi as ‘mind not immersed in samādhi’?
translation_text.sn12.70:7.16: Do you understand freed mind as ‘freed mind’,
translation_text.sn12.70:8.1: “Well, knowing and seeing thus, do you recollect many kinds of past lives? That is: one, two, three, four, five, ten, twenty, thirty, forty, fifty, a hundred, a thousand, a hundred thousand rebirths; many eons of the world contracting, many eons of the world expanding, many eons of the world contracting and expanding? Do you remember: ‘There, I was named this, my clan was that, I looked like this, and that was my food. This was how I felt pleasure and pain, and that was how my life ended. When I passed away from that place I was reborn somewhere else. There, too, I was named this, my clan was that, I looked like this, and that was my food. This was how I felt pleasure and pain, and that was how my life ended. When I passed away from that place I was reborn here.’ Do you recollect your many kinds of past lives, with features and details?”
translation_text.sn12.70:9.1: “Well, knowing and seeing thus, do you, with clairvoyance that is purified and superhuman, see sentient beings passing away and being reborn—inferior and superior, beautiful and ugly, in a good place or a bad place—and understand how sentient beings pass on according to their deeds? ‘These dear beings did bad things by way of body, speech, and mind. They denounced the noble ones; they had wrong view; and they chose to act out of that wrong view. When their body breaks up, after death, they’re reborn in a place of loss, a bad place, the underworld, hell. These dear beings, however, did good things by way of body, speech, and mind. They never denounced the noble ones; they had right view; and they chose to act out of that right view. When their body breaks up, after death, they’re reborn in a good place, a heavenly realm.’ And so, with clairvoyance that is purified and superhuman, do you see sentient beings passing away and being reborn—inferior and superior, beautiful and ugly, in a good place or a bad place. And do you understand how sentient beings pass on according to their deeds?”
translation_text.sn12.70:10.1: “Well, knowing and seeing thus, do you have direct meditative experience of the peaceful liberations that are formless, transcending form?”
translation_text.sn12.70:11.1: “Well now, venerables, how could there be such a declaration when these things are not attained?”
translation_text.sn12.70:11.2: “Reverend Susīma, we are freed by wisdom.”
translation_text.sn12.70:12.1: “I don’t understand the detailed meaning of what you have said in brief.
translation_text.sn12.70:12.2: Please teach me this matter so I can understand the detailed meaning.”
translation_text.sn12.70:12.3: “Reverend Susīma, whether you understand or not, we are freed by wisdom.”
translation_text.sn12.70:13.1: Then Susīma went to the Buddha, bowed, sat down to one side,
translation_text.sn12.70:13.2: and informed the Buddha of all he had discussed with those mendicants.
translation_text.sn12.70:13.3: “Susīma, first comes knowledge of the stability of natural principles. Afterwards there is knowledge of extinguishment.”
translation_text.sn12.70:14.1: “Sir, I don’t understand the detailed meaning of what you have said in brief.
translation_text.sn12.70:14.2: Please teach me this matter so I can understand the detailed meaning.”
translation_text.sn12.70:14.3: “Susīma, whether you understand or not, first comes knowledge of the stability of natural principles. Afterwards there is knowledge of extinguishment.
translation_text.sn12.70:15.4: “But if it’s impermanent, is it suffering or happiness?”
translation_text.sn12.70:15.6: “But if it’s impermanent, suffering, and perishable, is it fit to be regarded thus:
translation_text.sn12.70:15.7: ‘This is mine, I am this, this is my self’?”
translation_text.sn12.70:15.11: “But if it’s impermanent, is it suffering or happiness?”
translation_text.sn12.70:15.13: “But if it’s impermanent, suffering, and perishable, is it fit to be regarded thus:
translation_text.sn12.70:15.14: ‘This is mine, I am this, this is my self’?”
translation_text.sn12.70:15.20: “But if they’re impermanent, are they suffering or happiness?”
translation_text.sn12.70:15.22: “But if they’re impermanent, suffering, and perishable, are they fit to be regarded thus:
translation_text.sn12.70:15.23: ‘This is mine, I am this, this is my self’?”
translation_text.sn12.70:15.27: “But if it’s impermanent, is it suffering or happiness?”
translation_text.sn12.70:15.29: “But if it’s impermanent, suffering, and perishable, is it fit to be regarded thus:
translation_text.sn12.70:15.30: ‘This is mine, I am this, this is my self’?”
translation_text.sn12.70:16.1: “So, Susīma, you should truly see any kind of form at all—past, future, or present; internal or external; solid or subtle; inferior or superior; far or near: all form—with right understanding: ‘This is not mine, I am not this, this is not my self.’
translation_text.sn12.70:16.2: You should truly see any kind of feeling at all—past, future, or present; internal or external; solid or subtle; inferior or superior; far or near: all feeling—with right understanding: ‘This is not mine, I am not this, this is not my self.’
translation_text.sn12.70:16.3: You should truly see any kind of perception at all—past, future, or present; internal or external; solid or subtle; inferior or superior; far or near: all perception—with right understanding: ‘This is not mine, I am not this, this is not my self.’
translation_text.sn12.70:16.4: You should truly see any kind of choices at all—past, future, or present; internal or external; solid or subtle; inferior or superior; far or near: all choices—with right understanding: ‘This is not mine, I am not this, this is not my self.’
translation_text.sn12.70:16.5: You should truly see any kind of consciousness at all—past, future, or present; internal or external; solid or subtle; inferior or superior; far or near: all consciousness—with right understanding: ‘This is not mine, I am not this, this is not my self.’
translation_text.sn12.70:17.1: Seeing this, a learned noble disciple grows disillusioned with form, feeling, perception, choices, and consciousness.
translation_text.sn12.70:17.2: Being disillusioned, desire fades away. When desire fades away they’re freed. When they’re freed, they know they’re freed.
translation_text.sn12.70:17.3: They understand: ‘Rebirth is ended, the spiritual journey has been completed, what had to be done has been done, there is nothing further for this place.’
translation_text.sn12.70:18.1: Susīma, do you see that rebirth is a requirement for old age and death?”
translation_text.sn12.70:18.3: “Do you see that continued existence is a requirement for rebirth?”
translation_text.sn12.70:18.5: “Do you see that grasping is a requirement for continued existence?”
translation_text.sn12.70:18.7: “Do you see that craving is a requirement for grasping?”
translation_text.sn12.70:18.9: “Do you see that feeling is a requirement for craving …
translation_text.sn12.70:18.10: contact is a requirement for feeling …
translation_text.sn12.70:18.11: the six sense fields are requirements for contact …
translation_text.sn12.70:18.12: name and form are requirements for the six sense fields …
translation_text.sn12.70:18.13: consciousness is a requirement for name and form …
translation_text.sn12.70:18.14: choices are a requirement for consciousness …
translation_text.sn12.70:18.15: ignorance is a requirement for choices?”
translation_text.sn12.70:19.1: “Do you see that when rebirth ceases old age and death cease?”
translation_text.sn12.70:19.3: “Do you see that when continued existence ceases rebirth ceases?”
translation_text.sn12.70:19.5: “Do you see that when grasping ceases continued existence ceases …
translation_text.sn12.70:19.9: when the six sense fields cease, contact ceases …
translation_text.sn12.70:19.10: when name and form cease, the six sense fields cease …
translation_text.sn12.70:19.11: when consciousness ceases name and form cease …
translation_text.sn12.70:20.1: “But knowing and seeing thus, do you wield the many kinds of psychic power? …”
translation_text.sn12.70:21.1: “Well, knowing and seeing thus do you, with clairaudience that is purified and superhuman, hear both kinds of sounds, human and heavenly, whether near or far?”
translation_text.sn12.70:22.1: “Well, knowing and seeing thus, do you understand the minds of other beings and individuals, having encompassed them with your mind? …”
translation_text.sn12.70:23.1: “Well, knowing and seeing thus, do you recollect many kinds of past lives, with features and details?”
translation_text.sn12.70:24.1: “Well, knowing and seeing thus, do you, with clairvoyance that is purified and superhuman, see sentient beings passing away and being reborn … according to their deeds?”
translation_text.sn12.70:25.1: “Well, knowing and seeing thus, do you have direct meditative experience of the peaceful liberations that are formless, transcending form?”
translation_text.sn12.70:26.1: “Well now, Susīma, how could there be such a declaration when these things are not attained?”
translation_text.sn12.70:27.1: Then Venerable Susīma bowed with his head at the Buddha’s feet and said,
translation_text.sn12.70:27.2: “I made a mistake, sir. It was foolish, stupid, and unskillful of me to go forth as a thief in such a well-explained teaching and training.
translation_text.sn12.70:27.3: Please, sir, accept my mistake for what it is, so I will restrain myself in future.”
translation_text.sn12.70:28.1: “Indeed, Susīma, you made a mistake. It was foolish, stupid, and unskillful of you to go forth as a thief of the teachings in such a well-explained teaching and training.
translation_text.sn12.70:28.2: Suppose they were to arrest a bandit, a criminal and present him to the king, saying:
translation_text.sn12.70:28.3: ‘Your Majesty, this is a bandit, a criminal. Punish him as you will.’
translation_text.sn12.70:28.5: ‘Go, my men, and tie this man’s arms tightly behind his back with a strong rope. Shave his head and march him from street to street and from square to square to the beating of a harsh drum. Then take him out the south gate and there, to the south of the city, chop off his head.’
translation_text.sn12.70:28.6: The king’s men would do as they were told.
translation_text.sn12.70:28.8: Wouldn’t that man experience pain and distress because of that?”
translation_text.sn12.70:29.1: “Although that man would experience pain and distress because of that,
translation_text.sn12.70:29.2: going forth as a thief of the teachings in such a well-explained teaching and training has a more painful and bitter result. And it even leads to the underworld.
translation_text.sn12.70:29.3: But since you have recognized your mistake for what it is, and have dealt with it properly, I accept it.
translation_text.sn12.70:29.4: For it is growth in the training of the Noble One to recognize a mistake for what it is, deal with it properly, and commit to restraint in the future.”

variant_text.sn12.70:5.1: Api pana → api nu (bj, sya-all, km)
variant_text.sn12.70:29.1: paṭisaṁvediyetha → paṭisaṁvedayetha (bj, sya-all); patisaṁvediyetha vā na vā paṭisaṁvediyetha (pts1ed, pts2ed, mr)
variant_text.sn12.70:30.6: dasamo susimena cāti → dasamo vutto susīmenāti (bj); dasamo vutto susimenāti (sya-all); dasamo susīmena cāti (pts1ed, pts2ed)
